era: Hip-Hop. E=6 -- neighborhood hustle, Ballard enters the engine. Self-released since 2006. Relentless output. Fourteenth consecutive E=6 (161-174). v1.49.314.

location.formative: Ballard -- historically Scandinavian working-class neighborhood. Now gentrified. Hip-hop from unexpected geography. Community-built audience.

members.name: Grynch
members.note: Ballard, Seattle. Self-released hip-hop since 2006. Twenty years of independent output. Neighborhood hustle.

theory_nodes.concept: Neighborhood hustle as E=6 -- relentless output + self-released independence + community connection + twenty-year longevity + versatile craft. Hustle as force. One person as entire label.
examples: No label. Direct to audience. Self-built infrastructure.
Versatile catalog -- party tracks to introspective storytelling.
theory_nodes.description: Neighborhood hustle. E=6 through relentless independent output.

genealogy.relationship: Grynch at 174. Hustle as force. Fourteenth E=6 mode.

theory_nodes.concept: Ballard enters the engine -- new neighborhood. Historically Scandinavian, now gentrified. Hip-hop from unexpected geography. Expands the engine's neighborhood map.
examples: Central District (146/157/170/173): Black cultural center, Sub Pop.
Different neighborhoods produce different hip-hop modes.
Geography shapes artistic identity and infrastructure.
theory_nodes.description: Ballard enters. New neighborhood. Hip-hop from unexpected soil.

genealogy.relationship: Grynch at 174. First Ballard artist in engine.

theory_nodes.concept: Self-released economy deepening (164/169/172/174) -- four modes of independence. Aggressive underground, dark cinematic, underground persistence, neighborhood hustle. Independence as strategy.
theory_nodes.description: Self-released economy. Four modes. Independence deepening.

cross_references.relationship: SELF-RELEASED! Both self-released Seattle hip-hop. Underground persistence (172) vs neighborhood hustle (174). Same economy, different modes.
SELF-RELEASED! Dark cinematic (169) vs neighborhood hustle (174). Both self-released. Different aesthetics, same independence.
GEOGRAPHIC CONTRAST! Central District + Sub Pop (173) vs Ballard + self-released (174). Different neighborhoods, different labels, same E=6.
HUSTLE CONNECTION! Both built careers through direct community connection and relentless output. Small-town hunger (166) vs neighborhood hustle (174).
